title: podman unable to build on M2 mac
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

When trying to build using podman on an M2 mac the build process fails with the following error:

```
zozo@Peters-MacBook-Pro ~ % podman build -t quay.io/zozo6015/openjdk:11 --rm --no-cache .
ERRO[0303] 1 error occurred:
	* archive/tar: write too long

 
Error: Post "http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=3&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nocache=1&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11": io: read/write on closed pipe
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->


1. Install podman using brew or package on an M2 mac. 

2. Create a Dockerfile with this content:
```
FROM	alpine:3
LABEL	description="java 11"
ENV	TZ=UTC
RUN	apk add --no-cache bash openjdk11 curl
CMD	["bash", "-c", "while true; do date; sleep 5; done"]
```
3.  Initialize the podman machine using:
```
podman machine init
```

4. run the build:
```
podman build -t [quay.io/zozo6015/openjdk:11](http://quay.io/zozo6015/openjdk:11) . -f Dockerfile
```
**Describe the results you received:**
After waiting quite some time the build process errors out with the following message:
```
ERRO[0303] 1 error occurred:
	* archive/tar: write too long

 
Error: Post "http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=3&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nocache=1&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11": io: read/write on closed pipe
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
The image should be built and should be listed using `podman images`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
if I ssh into the podman machine and run the build in there everything works as expected.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.8
Built:        Wed Nov  9 22:43:58 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Nov 11 17:00:31 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
running on an M2 macbook pro.

comments.body: I'm unable to reproduce on M1 and don't have access to an M2 machine. Just in case, could you try rm-ing and re-initing a new machine and trying the build again? Occasionally that helps.

```
podman machine ls
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED       LAST UP       CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default*  qemu        43 hours ago  43 hours ago  1           2.147GB     107.4GB
```

```
podman machine rm

The following files will be deleted:

/Users/zozo/.ssh/podman-machine-default
/Users/zozo/.ssh/podman-machine-default.pub
/Users/zozo/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.ign
/Users/zozo/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_fedora-coreos-37.20221127.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2
/Users/zozo/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock
/Users/zozo/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default_ovmf_vars.fd
/Users/zozo/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json


Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
```

```
podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start

❯ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
WARN[0000] podman helper is installed, but was not able to claim the global docker sock 
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users/zozo:/Users/zozo

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command: 

	podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding listening on: /Users/zozo/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock

Another process was listening on the default Docker API socket address.
You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

	export DOCKER_HOST='unix:///Users/zozo/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/podman-machine-default/podman.sock'

Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully
```

```
podman build -t quay.io/zozo6015/openjdk:11 . -f Dockerfile
ERRO[0136] 1 error occurred:
	* archive/tar: write too long

 
Error: Post "http://d/v4.3.1/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&identitylabel=1&idmappingoptions=%7B%22HostUIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22HostGIDMapping%22%3Atrue%2C%22UIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22GIDMap%22%3A%5B%5D%2C%22AutoUserNs%22%3Afalse%2C%22AutoUserNsOpts%22%3A%7B%22Size%22%3A0%2C%22InitialSize%22%3A0%2C%22PasswdFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22GroupFile%22%3A%22%22%2C%22AdditionalUIDMappings%22%3Anull%2C%22AdditionalGIDMappings%22%3Anull%7D%7D&isolation=3&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&omithistory=0&output=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=quay.io%2Fzozo6015%2Fopenjdk%3A11": io: read/write on closed pipe
```
Did that several times always on the same result on my M2 MacBook Pro.
any chance you have large objects or lots of little ones in the context dir?
context dir contained only the Dockerfile nothing else
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@zozo6015 Do you still have this issue?
yes, it only works if I ssh into the pod man machine and I build there.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Not sure what happened but podman was able to build the same version that before wasn't.
I think this means I can close this issue, reopen if I am mistaken.
